content:
Hi! I'm creating a new branch, tyr-dev, that rebases tyr-next onto drm-rust-next, so that we can build on the part of the Tyr driver that is now upstream. It's still a WIP because today I am still checking that it will compile and run.

@Beata Michalska I heard from @Daniel Almeida you might have changes that would otherwise go to tyr-next but if you wanted to wait until tyr-dev is functional that might be even better, although I could work around either approch.

Here's a link to the rebase issue:
https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/issues/56

@Deborah Brouwer btw I am not sure it makes sense to have yet another branch. Once you're done, it might make sense to simply force-push tyr-next to that and delete your branch.

@Beata Michalska @Alice Ryhl wdyt?
I was thinking... you might want to reuse the tyr-next branch fo commits that could go directly upstream, and then use the tyr-dev branch for commits that rely on the downstream stuff?

might work as well, let's hear from others

cc @Boris Brezillon
I honestly don't mind, as long as it's documented somewhere :D
I think tyr-next is already a mix of both , but I do not mind either approach
@Deborah Brouwer Fine Deb, this didn't occur to me. Go ahead and make three branches then
ok, i've force pushed a new version of tyr-dev but it still has compile errors :cry: . A lot of them have to do with the register read and write functions requiring a dev: &Device<Bound>
We can discuss this on a call if needed but tl;dr is: you only get a Bound device as the argument of some specific functions, like probe for example
Or anywhere else where the kernel knows that the device is actually bound. Otherwise you have to use the more expensive “try_access()” method on Revocable instead of “access()”
Read up on Rust typestates btw
try_access works with any &dev::Device
You’ll need to add new read/write variants that use try_access underneath. Btw try naming them try_read and try_write
thanks @Daniel Almeida I understand what you're saying and adding try_read try_write to Register works just fine to get it compiled

@Daniel Almeida, @Deborah Brouwer What's the scope you're running into the case that you can't prove to have a &Device<Bound>?

If it's DRM IOCTLs, that's expected and something I am planning on addressing in the DRM C code in the future.

If it's somewhere else, the correct solution most likely is not to use try_acccess().

Hi @Danilo Krummrich no it wasn’t in the DRM IOCTL context, it was just in the irqs. And I noticed later that since the handle method of ThreadedHandler now takes a bound device argument, we can pass the bound device through that way without using try_read/try_write

Update on this, tyr-dev is rebased on drm-rust-next for v6.18-rc2 and it's compiling and running, but i've had to make so many changes to the driver in tyr-next, for it to rebase on the upstream Tyr driver, i feel like i should make separate commits so that the changes are more transparent for discussion. So, I'll work on unsquishing the commits a bit better next.

Update: I've made tyr-dev equivalent to drm-rust-next and then opened a series of MR to rebase tyr-next onto upstream tyr-driver. MRs and their order are all summarized here: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/issues/56
^ Sorry, was a bit busy with our IGT tests. Will have a look at your MRs tomorrow.

@Daniel Almeida I think i've addressed all of your comments now, so when you're able, please have a look again at these remaining MRs:
tyr-kconfig !39
from-tyr-next !40
tyr-fixups !41
vmap-changes !42
tyr-data-arc !46

Deborah Brouwer said:

Daniel Almeida I think i've addressed all of your comments now, so when you're able, please have a look again at these remaining MRs:
tyr-kconfig !39
from-tyr-next !40
tyr-fixups !41
vmap-changes !42
tyr-data-arc !46

@Daniel Almeida Since tyr-mini-demo has a bunch of development work, I'm adding on to the end of this chain:
tyr-mini-demo-rebased !48
Hi Deb, let’s test it next week :)
Thanks for this

@Daniel Almeida i think we're getting closer to having a rebased tyr-dev work from. Here's what's left: for your review:
vmap-changes !42
tyr-data-arc !46
tyr-mini-demo-rebased !48

@Daniel Almeida do you think we are closer to reconciling tyr-mini-demo and tyr-dev ? Here's what I think we need to merge now to get the mini-demo working at parity on the upstreamed driver as of v6.18-rc2

  1. tyr: add new work on tyr-mini-demo branch
    https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/51

  2. tyr: clean up warnings
    https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/50

  3. tyr: add dev_query types
    https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/panfrost/linux/-/merge_requests/52
This is done now and tyr-dev is ready to use.
Deborah Brouwer has marked this topic as resolved.
